expressions.korean: 수면 부족
expressions.english: lack of sleep; sleep deprivation

expressions.korean: 경험 부족
expressions.english: lack of experience

expressions.korean: 운동 부족
expressions.english: lack of exercise

expressions.korean: 부족하다
expressions.english: to lack; to be short of; to be insufficient

expressions.korean: 음식 안 부족해요?
expressions.english: Would you like more food?

expressions.korean: 여기 의자가 하나 부족해요.
expressions.english: We are short of a chair here.
expressions.notes:
여기 = here, this place
의자 = chair

expressions.korean: 부족한 게 있으면 말씀하세요.
expressions.english:
If you are lacking anything, tell us.
Let us know if there's a shortage of anything.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 = if/when + S + V, once + S + V
말씀하다 = (honorific) to talk; to say; to speak; to tell

expressions.korean: 요즘 잠이 너무 부족해서 피곤해요.
expressions.english: I am tired because I am really lacking sleep these days.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
잠 = sleep

expressions.korean: 부족한 게 많지만, 열심히 준비했어요.
expressions.english: I am lacking a lot, but I diligently prepared.
expressions.notes:
열심히 = (to do something) hard; diligently
준비하다 = to prepare; to get ready

expressions.korean: 이번 프로젝트는 준비가 너무 부족해요.
expressions.english: This project lacks a lot of preparation.

expressions.korean: 하고 싶은 일은 많은데 시간이 부족해요.
expressions.english: I have a lot of things I want to do, but there is not a sufficient amount of time.
expressions.notes:
하다 = to do
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion

expressions.korean: 꼭 사고 싶은 책이 있는데 돈이 부족해요.
expressions.english: There is a book I really want to buy, but I am short of money.
expressions.notes:
자리 = space, spot, seat, position; occasion
-(으)니까 = since, because
옆 = next
-이/이/어 주세요. = (polite) Please do + something + for me.

expressions.korean: 자리가 부족하니까 옆으로 좀 비켜 주세요.
expressions.english: There's not enough space, so please move to the side a little.
expressions.notes:
노래 = song
잘하다 = to be good at something
자신감 = confidence

expressions.korean: 경은 씨는 노래는 잘하는데 자신감이 부족해요.
expressions.english: Kyeong-eun sings well, but lacks self-confidence.
expressions.notes:
보고서 = report
완성하다 = to finish, complete, get something done
자료 = data; material

expressions.korean: 보고서를 완성해야 하는데 자료가 너무 부족해요.
expressions.english: I have to finish the report, but the data is really insufficient.
expressions.notes:
-다고 해서 = since someone says/said ...
행복하다 = to be happy

expressions.korean: 돈이 부족하다고 해서 행복하지 않은 건 아니에요.
expressions.english: Just because one has an insufficient amount of money doesn't mean one is unhappy.
expressions.notes:
머리 = head; hair
아프다 = to be sick, hurt
병원 = hospital
-다니 = S + V-ed + and as a result
원인 = cause
-래요 = I heard ...; They said ...

expressions.korean: 머리가 아파서 병원에 갔더니 수면 부족이 원인이래요.
expressions.english: I went to the hospital because my head hurt, and they said the cause was lack of sleep.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
실력 = skill, ability
아직 = (not) yet
어리다 = to be young

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 실력은 있는데 아직 어려서 경험이 부족해요.
expressions.english: That person has the skills, but lacks experience since he is still young.
expressions.notes:
고백 = confession
고백을 하다 = to confess
-(으)려고 하다 = to plan to + V; to think of + V-ing; to mean to + V
용기 = courage

expressions.korean: 오늘은 꼭 고백을 하려고 했는데 용기가 부족해서 못 했어요.
expressions.english: I really wanted to confess (my feelings) today, but I couldn't because I lacked the courage.
expressions.notes:
컴퓨터 = computer
저장하다 = to save
용량 = capacity; volume

expressions.korean: 제 컴퓨터에 저장하려고 했는데 용량이 부족해서 못 했어요.
expressions.english: I was going to save it on my computer, but I couldn't because there was insufficient memory.
